description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a military olive-green fuselage and two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, each adorned with roundel insignias. The aircraft features a single open cockpit positioned just behind the upper wing, with a visible propeller mounted at the nose. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer with a tricolor rudder and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The fuselage is reinforced with visible struts and rigging wires connecting the wings and tail. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft, likely from the World War I era.
description: A vintage olive-green biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, roundel insignias, and fixed landing gear. Features a front-mounted propeller and a tricolor tail rudder.
description_view_0: Oblique rear-side view showing the roundel insignias on the wings, the tricolor tail rudder, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Direct top-down view highlighting the parallel biplane wings, roundel insignias, and the full span of the tail section.
description_view_2: Oblique rear-side view from the opposite angle, again showing the insignias, tail rudder, and cockpit placement.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the propeller, open cockpit, and the struts and rigging wires between the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets; slight dihedral on lower wing; extensive strut and wire bracing.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers; tricolor rudder.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no canopy.
